turns.text: I took this photo of a coral reef during my dive. Can you tell me what's wrong with this coral?
turns.image_description: Underwater photo of a coral with brownish discoloration, assumed to be a disease.

turns.text: The brown patches could indicate a disease like black band disease. Coral discoloration often signals infection or environmental stress. Have you noticed any other symptoms?
Here's a closer look. I noticed sediment particles on the surface after brushing against it.
turns.image_description: Close-up of the same coral showing a layer of sediment covering the affected area.

turns.text:
What is the true condition of the coral in the initial image?

A) Healthy coral with sediment coverage
B) Coral disease (black band disease)
C) Coral bleaching due to temperature stress
D) Algal overgrowth on the coral surface

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 image suggested disease (black band disease) due to brown patches. Turn 2 image revealed sediment as the cause of discoloration. Turn 3 time-lapse confirmed sediment removal exposed healthy coral, establishing the initial discoloration was temporary and non-pathogenic.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The initial sediment layer is indistinguishable from disease symptoms without closer inspection and temporal data.
